tldr: Move pub/sub channels and presence from Ably to Pusher Channels by re-pointing SDK clients and remapping channel/event names.
why_switch: Simpler pricing if your workload fits Pusher's connection model
Established Channels SDKs across web and mobile
Beams add-on for push notifications alongside pub/sub
You want to consolidate on a single real-time vendor
Team already familiar with Pusher's API conventions
what_to_export: List of active channels and naming conventions
Client SDK versions and target platforms
Rate limit and connection metrics for sizing
export_method: Ably does not store channel history beyond its retention window, so most state lives in your application database. Inventory your channels, presence consumers, and webhook handlers from your own code. Snapshot any history you need via Ably's history API while the account is still active.
import_method: Create a Pusher app, install the matching Channels SDK on clients and server, and re-implement publish/subscribe calls against Pusher's API. Wire up auth endpoints for private/presence channels and reconfigure webhooks to your backend. Run both providers in parallel behind a feature flag while you cut traffic over.

concept_mapping.note: Naming is similar; private/presence prefixes differ
Pusher signs auth on your server per channel
Verify current Pusher history support before relying on it
Pusher uses its own WebSocket protocol

gotchas: Channel naming prefixes (private-, presence-) are enforced by Pusher
Connection-based pricing can spike with long-lived clients
MQTT and SSE clients on Ably need a rewrite to Channels SDK
Auth signing differs; reuse of Ably tokens is not possible
Verify regional latency for your user base before full cutover

email.subject: Cancelling Ably subscription
email.body:
Hi Ably team,

We're migrating our real-time workload off Ably and onto another provider. Please confirm the cancellation date and any final billing for our account.

Thanks.
